mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE falls below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during late-cycle expansions triggers inflation expectations and anticipated monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of late-cycle observations, or Rank IC remains negative across all regime classifications.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.3% signals deteriorating purchasing power and forces earlier central bank tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.3% MoM are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth rates above 2.0% reflect sustained economic expansion that directly boosts corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% is followed by negative 5-day returns in > 55% of observations, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Surging retail sales during a monetary tightening cycle amplify inflationary pressures and accelerate rate hike expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS surges during tightening periods yield positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the regime-conditional effect disappears when FEDFUNDS is rising.
